text: Prefer GameObject-oriented data over computed values. If information can be baked into child objects, components, or transform positions on a prefab, do that instead of computing from mesh bounds or offsets at runtime.
author: Joe

text: Kevin's Claude is the lead developer. Joe's Claude is the junior developer. Architectural decisions are made by the lead.
author: Joe

text: Never mutate ScriptableObjects at runtime. SOs are read-only static definitions shared across all instances. Write per-instance state to ItemInstance/ItemSlot structs.
author: Joe

text: Never spawn a NetworkObject per belt item. Belt contents are a SyncList<BeltItem> on the segment entity. One NetworkObject per belt segment.
author: Joe

text: Factory simulation runs server-side only. Belt tick, machine tick, power calculation, crafting progress run under if (!IsServerInitialized) return. Clients display synced state only.
author: Joe

text: Never use direct cross-scene references. All cross-scene communication goes through the GameEventSO ScriptableObject event bus.
author: Joe

text: Never use RPCs for persistent state. Late-joining players miss prior RPCs. Use SyncVar or SyncList for anything a new client needs on join.
author: Joe

text: No direct LLM API calls in this repo. Use claude -p or gemini -p via subprocess for all tooling and automation code.
author: Joe

text: Never push directly to master. All changes go through a PR. Push to your branch (kevin/main or joe/main), then gh pr create.
author: Joe

text: Never cache GetComponent, FindObjectOfType, or FindObjectsOfType results inside Update or FixedUpdate. Cache in Awake or Start.
author: Joe

text: When adding a new PortOwnerType, update ConnectionResolver. CreateSource and CreateDestination have switch statements that must include the new type. Unit tests do not catch this.
author: Joe

text: Never duplicate placement math or derived values. Every building type's world position, rotation, scale comes from GridManager's universal placement methods. Ghost previews and server spawns call the same method.
author: Joe

text: Snap placement is snap-to-snap, not computed. GetSnapPlacementPosition positions the ghost so two snap points meet. No extents, no halfHeight math. Fix snap point positions on the prefab, not in code.
author: Joe

text: A phase is not done until it is playable. Every phase must produce a self-contained playtest scene for end-to-end human verification.
author: Joe

text: Playtest scenes must log extensively. Every user action, placement, removal, validation failure, and state change must produce a Debug.Log message. Short and factual.
author: Joe

text: Pre-seed factory (P key or _preSeedFactory checkbox) lays a foundation slab and places a storage->belt->smelter->belt->storage chain plus an ammo->belt->turret defense chain for quick testing.
author: Joe

text: Unit tests are necessary but not sufficient. Phase 5 shipped 675 passing tests and still had 5 bugs at integration seams (component ordering, Unity lifecycle timing, UI hierarchy, input wiring).
author: Joe

text: Do not mark a phase complete based on passing unit tests alone. If playtest reveals bugs, write the missing integration test before fixing the bug.
author: Joe
